title: L4: Game Creation
description: Build three progressively complex Scratch games applying variables, conditionals, and loops: a Pac-Man style game, an underwater game, and a game of your own choice.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now

Which script makes a sprite chase another sprite called Player?
steps.js: createMCQ('gm-dna',['when flag clicked → move (3) steps','when flag clicked → forever → point towards [Player v] → move (3) steps','when flag clicked → go to [Player v]','when flag clicked → if touching [Player v] → move steps'],1,'Correct — point towards + move inside a forever loop is the standard chase pattern.','forever → point towards [Player] → move steps makes the sprite always face and follow the player.');

steps.title: Key Game Techniques
steps.content:
Key techniques for all three games

Here are the patterns you will combine. Read them — you will use every one.

— Player movement —
forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then → change x by (5)
  if <key [left arrow v] pressed?> then → change x by (-5)
end
— Sprite chases player —
forever
  point towards [Player v]
  move (3) steps
end
— Collect and reset —
forever
  if <touching [Player v]?> then
    go to x: (pick random (-200) to (200)) y: (pick random (-150) to (150))
    change [score v] by (1)
  end
end
— Enemy catches player, score drops —
forever
  point towards [Player v]
  move (2) steps
  if <touching [Player v]?> then
    change [score v] by (-1)
    say [Got you!] for (1) seconds
  end
end

steps.title: Game 1: Pac-Man
steps.content:
Game 1: Pac-Man

  1. Delete the cat. Paint a maze background (rectangle tool, two colours).
  2. Paint a Pac-Man: a yellow circle. Duplicate the costume and rub a wedge from the second. Resize it to fit the maze.
  3. Pac-Man script: forever → four arrow key if blocks (change x/y by ±5).
  4. Create a Score variable. Set to 0 on flag.
  5. Add a reward sprite (search Bananas or draw a dot). Script: forever → if touching Pacman → go to random position + change Score by 1.
  6. Add a chaser sprite. Script: forever → point towards Pacman → move 2 steps.
steps.js: (function(){var NW=1100,NH=650;function sf(){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(!f)return;var w=f.parentElement,bar=w.querySelector('.y7-check-bar'),bH=bar?bar.offsetHeight:44,fs=w.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'),aW=fs?window.innerWidth:w.clientWidth,sc=fs?Math.min(aW/NW,(window.innerHeight-bH)/NH):aW/NW;f.style.width=NW+'px';f.style.height=NH+'px';f.style.transform='scale('+sc+')';f.style.marginBottom=Math.round(NH*(sc-1))+'px';f.style.marginLeft=fs?Math.max(0,Math.round((aW-NW*sc)/2))+'px':'0';}function tfs(){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),w=f.parentElement,b=document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn'),fs=w.classList.toggle('y7-fullscreen');b.textContent=fs?'✕':'⛶';if(fs){window._sy=window.scrollY;document.body.style.overflow='hidden';}else{document.body.style.overflow='';window.scrollTo(0,window._sy||0);}sf();}sf();if(window._sfR)window.removeEventListener('resize',window._sfR);window._sfR=sf;window.addEventListener('resize',sf);if(window._sfK)document.removeEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);window._sfK=function(e){if(e.key==='Escape'){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))tfs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=tfs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){runStepRuntimeTest({kind:'keyMoves',key:'ArrowRight',axis:'x',direction:'positive'},'✓ Arrow key controls working!','Arrow key not moving the sprite — check forever loop with if right arrow pressed → change x by (5).');};})()

steps.title: Game 2: Underwater
steps.content:
Game 2: Underwater Game

  1. Add the Underwater 1 backdrop. Delete the cat.
  2. Add Diver1 sprite. Costumes tab → add Diver2. Diver script: forever → arrow keys (change x/y) → next costume.
  3. Create Score variable. Draw a Jewel sprite. Script: forever → if touching Diver1 → go to random position + change Score by 1.
  4. Add Shark2. Script: forever → point towards Diver1 → move 2 steps → if touching Diver1 → change Score by -1 → say [Yum Yum!] for 1 second.

steps.title: Game 3: Your Choice
steps.content:
Game 3: make your own

Find a game you like on Scratch.mit.edu and recreate it, or invent your own. It must use at least three of these features:

  • A background that fits the theme
  • A sprite that animates (next costume in a loop)
  • Arrow key player control
  • A score variable that increases
  • A chasing enemy (point towards + move)
  • A sprite that resets to a random position when touched

quizQuestions.q: Which script makes a sprite chase another called 'Player'?
options: forever → point towards [Player v] → move (3) steps

quizQuestions.q: How do you make a collectible reappear in a random place when touched?
options: go to x: (pick random -240 to 240) y: (pick random -180 to 180)

quizQuestions.q: How do you decrease the score by 1?
Which script adds 1 to score when the sprite touches 'Jewel'?
Build a sprite that chases the mouse pointer in a forever loop. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: when flag clicked → forever → point towards mouse-pointer → move 3 steps

quizQuestions.q: Build a collectible sprite that jumps to a random position and increases the score when touched by another sprite. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: forever → if touching [Player]? → go to x: random y: random → change [score] by 1

quizQuestions.q: Build a script with arrow key control: right arrow moves the sprite right, left arrow moves it left, all inside a forever loop. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: forever → if right pressed → change x 5 → if left pressed → change x -5
